repo_notes.content: DefenseClaw is a security governance layer for OpenClaw and agentic AI runtimes. It scans skills, MCP servers, plugins, and agent-generated code before use; inspects prompts, completions, and tool calls at runtime; enforces policy through block/allow actions; and exports durable audit evidence through SQLite, JSONL, OTLP, Splunk, webhooks, and a TUI.
repo_notes.author: Maintainer

repo_notes.content:
The codebase is organized into these main areas:
- cli/defenseclaw/ - Python operator CLI, config loading, scanner wrappers, observability setup, webhook helpers, and commands.
- cmd/defenseclaw/ - Go gateway executable entry point.
- internal/gateway/ - Go sidecar, REST API, WebSocket bridge, guardrail proxy, provider adapters, inspection pipeline, hooks, webhooks, and gateway events.
- internal/scanner/ - Go scanner wrappers and normalized scan result types.
- internal/audit/, internal/gatewaylog/, internal/telemetry/ - audit persistence, structured runtime logs, metrics, traces, and schema emission.
- internal/tui/ - terminal dashboard for alerts, scans, tools, policy, setup, observability, and health.
- internal/policy/, policies/rego/, policies/guardrail/, policies/scanners/ - policy evaluation, Rego modules, guardrail rule packs, suppressions, and scanner profiles.
- extensions/defenseclaw/ - OpenClaw TypeScript plugin, fetch interception, tool-call inspection hooks, provider detection, and plugin tests.
- bundles/ - package-local local observability and Splunk bridge assets.
- docs/ - central Markdown documentation.
repo_notes.author: Maintainer

repo_notes.content: Documentation should prioritize practical operator flows: installation, quick start, scanner usage, guardrail setup and tuning, sandbox setup, observability, Splunk/local Grafana, TUI operation, policy authoring, plugin development, and testing. Keep durable Markdown docs under docs/ and keep package-local READMEs beside bundles or examples that need local context.
repo_notes.author: Maintainer

repo_notes.content: Important source-of-truth values: default gateway API port is 18970, default guardrail proxy port is 4000, Go version is 1.26.2, Python supports 3.10+, package version is in pyproject.toml and Makefile, and the local config directory is ~/.defenseclaw/.
repo_notes.author: Maintainer

repo_notes.content: Do not create dedicated wiki pages for local caches, generated build output, node_modules, .venv, .gocache, tmp, .cursor, .devin, or cli/defenseclaw/_data generated bundle copies. Mention these only when explaining build, packaging, or docs workflows.
repo_notes.author: Maintainer

pages.title: Home
pages.purpose: High-level overview of DefenseClaw: what it protects, the governance problem it solves, the Python CLI + Go gateway + OpenClaw plugin architecture, key capabilities, limitations, and links to the rest of the wiki. Reference README.md, docs/README.md, pyproject.toml, Makefile, and go.mod.

pages.title: Key Concepts and Security Model
pages.purpose: Explain core concepts: skills, MCP servers, plugins, agent-generated code, admission control, observe vs action mode, scan verdicts, block/allow policy, runtime guardrails, sandboxing, audit evidence, and the limits of automated security controls. Reference docs/ARCHITECTURE.md, docs/GUARDRAIL.md, docs/SANDBOX.md, internal/policy/, and policies/.

pages.title: Quick Start
pages.purpose: Step-by-step setup for a local developer or operator: prerequisites, install, defenseclaw init --enable-guardrail, defenseclaw doctor, basic skill/MCP/plugin scans, starting the gateway, launching the TUI, and where logs/audit data appear. Reference README.md, docs/QUICKSTART.md, docs/INSTALL.md, cli/defenseclaw/commands/cmd_quickstart.py, and cli/defenseclaw/commands/cmd_init.py.

pages.title: Installation and Configuration
pages.purpose: Comprehensive install and config guide: source install, Makefile targets, release script, PATH setup, config directory, config.yaml, .env secret loading, scanner policy defaults, guardrail settings, gateway ports, and provider configuration. Reference docs/INSTALL.md, docs/CONFIG_FILES.md, Makefile, scripts/install.sh, cli/defenseclaw/config.py, internal/config/, and policies/.

pages.title: Architecture
pages.purpose: System architecture for the Python CLI, Go gateway sidecar, OpenClaw plugin, policy engine, scanner wrappers, guardrail proxy, audit store, telemetry exporters, and sandbox integration. Include component boundaries and request/data flow. Reference docs/ARCHITECTURE.md, docs/reference/GATEWAY_SPEC.md, internal/gateway/, internal/scanner/, internal/audit/, internal/telemetry/, and extensions/defenseclaw/src/.

pages.title: CLI and Gateway Commands
pages.purpose: Operator guide for defenseclaw CLI commands and defenseclaw-gateway commands: init, doctor, setup, skill, mcp, plugin, codeguard, policy, audit, alerts, tui, sandbox, status, start/stop/restart, scan, watchdog, and provenance. Reference docs/CLI.md, internal/cli/, cli/defenseclaw/commands/, and cmd/defenseclaw/main.go.

pages.title: Scanners and CodeGuard
pages.purpose: Explain scanner surfaces and result normalization: skill scanner, MCP scanner, plugin scanner, AIBOM/inventory, CodeGuard static analysis, scan policies, findings, severities, SARIF/JSON-style outputs, and how scans feed admission decisions and audit events. Reference docs/CLI.md, docs/PLUGINS.md, skills/codeguard/, cli/defenseclaw/scanner/, internal/scanner/, policies/scanners/, and schemas/scan-result.json.

pages.title: Runtime Guardrail
pages.purpose: Complete guide to the guardrail proxy and runtime inspection: provider adapters, prompt/completion inspection, tool call inspection, rule packs, suppressions, verdict cache, LLM judge, Cisco inspection, notification queue, action vs observe mode, and tuning false positives. Reference docs/GUARDRAIL.md, docs/GUARDRAIL_RULE_PACKS.md, docs/GUARDRAIL_QUICKSTART.md, internal/gateway/guardrail.go, internal/gateway/proxy.go, internal/gateway/inspect.go, internal/guardrail/, and policies/guardrail/.

pages.title: Admission Gate and Watcher
pages.purpose: Document filesystem watcher behavior, rescan/drift detection, policy actions, skill/MCP/plugin block and allow flows, quarantine/restore behavior, audit emission, and how gateway/client/plugin interactions enforce decisions. Reference internal/watcher/, internal/enforce/, internal/policy/, internal/gateway/sidecar.go, cli/defenseclaw/enforce/, and policies/rego/.

pages.title: Policy and Rego
pages.purpose: Guide to DefenseClaw policy layers: top-level YAML profiles, skill_actions, plugin_actions, MCP actions, guardrail config, scanner profiles, sandbox/firewall Rego modules, data files, hot reload, testing with opa, and parity checks. Reference docs/CONFIG_FILES.md, policies/, policies/rego/, internal/policy/, and internal/config/.
